ops.text: `task wasm` / `scripts/build-wasm.sh` calls bare `cargo`, which on a machine where Homebrew rust (`/opt/homebrew/bin/cargo`) precedes the rustup shim (`~/.cargo/bin/cargo`) on PATH resolves to Homebrew's cargo — it ignores the git-tracked `rust-toolchain.toml` pin and lacks wasm32-unknown-unknown std, so every crate fails with a confusingly-suppressed `E0463: can't find crate for core` (the `--message-format=json` in the script hides the human diagnostic behind a grep). `rustup target list --installed` misleadingly shows wasm32 present (that's the rustup toolchain, not the Homebrew one). Fix on this machine: put `~/.cargo/bin` ahead of `/opt/homebrew/bin` on PATH (or run `~/.cargo/bin/cargo`), then `task build`/`task test` pass. Same brew-shadows-version-manager shape as the stale-GOROOT mise/brew trap. The repo is correct (rust-toolchain.toml + bare cargo is the standard rustup contract); no repo change warranted — a rustup-specific workaround in the script would be defensive coding.
